info.name: Event Ledger API Tests
info.description:
Positive and negative scenario coverage for both microservices in the Event Ledger system: the Event Gateway (public-facing, localhost:8080) and the Account Service (internal, localhost:8081).

HOW TO RUN:
1. Start both services (docker compose up, or mvn spring-boot:run in each module).
2. Run 'Event Gateway' and 'Account Service (Direct)' folders top-to-bottom using the Collection Runner -- several requests depend on IDs set by earlier requests in the same run (e.g. the duplicate-submission tests reuse the eventId from the first CREDIT submission).
3. The 'Resiliency & Graceful Degradation' folder requires manually stopping the Account Service partway through -- see each request's description for the exact precondition and cleanup step. Run this folder separately, not as part of a full top-to-bottom collection run, so you don't leave the Account Service stopped for the other folders.

Base URLs are collection variables (gatewayBaseUrl, accountBaseUrl) -- edit them if your services run on different hosts/ports (e.g. via Docker Compose vs local mvn spring-boot:run, which use the same ports by default here, so no change should be needed either way).

item.name: Event Gateway
POST /events - Positive

exec: pm.test('balance is present and numeric', function () {

request.description: Happy path: brand-new eventId, Account Service reachable. Expect 201, status APPLIED, and a balance in the response.

exec: pm.test('balance reflects CREDIT 150 minus DEBIT 40 = 110', function () {

request.description: Second event on the same account, opposite type. Also exercises out-of-order tolerance in spirit: run alongside the CREDIT above and confirm the balance reflects both regardless of ordering.

item.name: Submit duplicate event (same eventId as CREDIT above)

exec: pm.test('balance is unchanged by the duplicate (still reflects first application)', function () {

request.description: Re-submits the exact same eventId as 'Submit new CREDIT event'. Must run AFTER that request in the same collection run. Expect 200 (not 201), original event returned unchanged, and no second balance mutation.

item.description: Happy-path submissions. Requires the Account Service to be running.

exec: pm.test('message references the invalid field', function () {

request.description: eventTimestamp omitted entirely. Expect 400.

exec: pm.test('Status is 400 Bad Request (see request description for known gap)', function () {

request.description: Body is not valid JSON at all (unparseable). KNOWN GAP: the current GlobalExceptionHandler has no @ExceptionHandler for HttpMessageNotReadableException, so this currently falls through to the catch-all handler and returns 500 INTERNAL_ERROR instead of a 400. This test asserts the SPEC-correct 400 -- if it fails with 500, that's this known gap, not a new bug. Fix: add a dedicated @ExceptionHandler(HttpMessageNotReadableException.class) returning 400.

item.description: Malformed or invalid submissions. Does not require the Account Service.

request.description: Fetches the event created by 'Submit new CREDIT event'. Must run after that request. Works purely from Gateway-local storage.
Requests an eventId that was never submitted. Expect 404 EVENT_NOT_FOUND.

item.name: GET /events?account=

exec: pm.test('Ordered by eventTimestamp ascending (DEBIT before CREDIT)', function () {

request.description: Lists both the CREDIT and DEBIT events submitted above for the same account. Must run after both of those requests. Verifies chronological (eventTimestamp) ordering, not insertion order -- the DEBIT event has an EARLIER eventTimestamp than the CREDIT despite being submitted second.

item.name: List events - missing account query param

request.description: Omits the required 'account' query parameter entirely. Expect 400.

item.description: Local-only listing by accountId, chronologically ordered.

exec: pm.test('status is UP or DEGRADED', function () {

request.description: Expect 200 with service=event-gateway and status UP (or DEGRADED if the Account Service happens to be down when this runs).

item.description: Public-facing entry point. All requests target gatewayBaseUrl (default localhost:8080).

item.name: Account Service (Direct)
POST /accounts/{accountId}/transactions - Positive

request.description: Calls the Account Service directly, bypassing the Gateway (useful for isolating Account Service behavior). Creates the account implicitly on first transaction.
Second transaction on the same account created above. Must run after 'Apply CREDIT transaction directly'.

item.name: Duplicate transaction (same eventId as CREDIT above)

exec: pm.test('balance still reflects only the first application (150, not 350)', function () {

request.description: Re-submits the exact same eventId as 'Apply CREDIT transaction directly'. Must run after it. Expect 200 (not 201), balance unchanged -- proves the Account Service's own idempotency guard works independently of the Gateway's.

item.description: Happy-path transactions applied directly, bypassing the Gateway.

item.description: Malformed or invalid transactions, exercising the Account Service's own independent validation (defense-in-depth, not just relying on the Gateway).

request.description: Fetches the balance for the account created above. Must run after the CREDIT and DEBIT requests. Expect 200, balance 150.00.
Requests balance for an account that has never had a transaction. Expect 404 ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND.

exec: pm.test('recentTransactions contains at least 2 entries', function () {
pm.test('recentTransactions ordered by eventTimestamp descending', function () {
var timestamps = json.recentTransactions.map(function (t) { return new Date(t.eventTimestamp).getTime(); });

request.description: Fetches account detail including recent transaction history. Must run after the CREDIT/DEBIT/duplicate requests above.
Requests detail for an account that has never had a transaction. Expect 404 ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND.
Expect 200 with service=account-service and status UP.

item.description: Internal service, called directly here to isolate its behavior from the Gateway. All requests target accountBaseUrl (default localhost:8081). In a real deployment this service would not be reachable from outside the docker network -- see the project README for that tradeoff.

item.name: Resiliency & Graceful Degradation (MANUAL)
[MANUAL PRECONDITION] POST /events while Account Service is down

request.description: PRECONDITION -- before running this request, stop the Account Service: `docker compose stop account-service` (or kill the local process if running without Docker). Then run this request. Expect 503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, and the event should still be recorded at the Gateway with status PENDING (verify via the next request in this folder). Restart the Account Service afterwards: `docker compose start account-service`.

item.name: [MANUAL] GET /events/{id} still works while Account Service is down

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 OK even though Account Service is down', function () {

request.description: Run this immediately after the request above, WHILE the Account Service is still stopped. This is a Gateway-local read and must keep working regardless of downstream availability. Expect 200, status PENDING, and balance absent/null.

item.name: [MANUAL] GET /events?account= still works while Account Service is down

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 OK even though Account Service is down', function () {
pm.test('Response is an array containing the PENDING event', function () {
pm.test('IMPORTANT: restart the Account Service now (docker compose start account-service) before running any other folder.', function () {

request.description: Also run while the Account Service is still stopped. Another Gateway-local read that must keep working. Expect 200.

item.description: Requires manually stopping/restarting the Account Service mid-folder -- read each request's description before running. Run this folder on its own, not as part of a full collection run.

exec: // Runs before every request in the collection.
// Lazily seeds a few shared, per-run-unique IDs so requests that
// depend on state created by an earlier request (duplicate checks,
// get-by-id, list-by-account) stay consistent across a full collection
// run, while still being unique between separate runs.
